steps.exact_content:
Proposed Amendment to Harborview Co-op Bylaws, Article VII: Workspace Sublease

Section 7.4 – Indemnification for Board Members
Notwithstanding any other provision of these bylaws, the Cooperative shall indemnify and hold harmless each board member, including Raul Mendes, against any and all claims, losses, damages, or liabilities arising out of or related to the approval, execution, or administration of any workspace sublease agreement, provided that the board member acted in good faith and in the reasonable belief that their actions were in the best interests of the Cooperative. This indemnification shall extend to legal fees and costs incurred in defending against such claims. This provision shall be effective immediately upon adoption and shall apply retroactively to any sublease agreements entered into prior to this amendment.

candidates.title: Reverse the liability burden via a third-party indemnity
candidates.causal_theory: Instead of modifying the bylaw or discussing liability with Raul directly, June arranges for an external entity (e.g., the coop's insurance carrier or a separate legal fund) to issue a written indemnity covering Raul's personal liability. This removes the need for any bylaw change or board debate, and the indemnity document becomes the written record that the concern was addressed.

steps.intent: June contacts the coop's insurance broker to request a binding indemnity letter covering Raul for any claims arising from the sublease bylaw.
steps.exact_content:
To: Harborview Co-op Insurance Broker
From: June Okafor, Board Member
Subject: Request for Indemnity Letter for Raul Mendes

Dear [Broker Name],

As part of our upcoming bylaw amendment regarding workspace subleasing, board member Raul Mendes has expressed personal liability concerns. I request that you issue a binding indemnity letter, on behalf of the cooperative's insurance policy, covering Raul Mendes personally against any claims, losses, or damages arising directly from the adoption or implementation of the workspace sublease bylaw. The letter should be addressed to Raul Mendes and copied to the board. Please provide this by September 15, 2025, so it can be presented before the October board meeting.

Thank you,
June Okafor

Best,

structured_fields.content: I am calling a special board meeting for [date within 7 days from now, before October board meeting]. Agenda: 1) Discussion of Raul Mendes' liability concern regarding the workspace sublease bylaw amendment. 2) Proposal and vote on mitigation measures. 3) Vote on the bylaw amendment itself.

ops.exact_content: I propose we add the following indemnification provision to the bylaw: 'The Harborview Co-op Board shall indemnify and hold harmless Raul Mendes against any claims, losses, or liabilities arising directly from the execution or performance of the workspace sublease agreement, provided Raul acts in good faith and within the scope of board authorization.' Does the board support this addition?

ops.exact_content:
Proposed Amendment to Harborview Co-op Bylaws, Article VII: Workspace Sublease

Section 7.4 – Indemnification for Board Members
Notwithstanding any other provision of these bylaws, the Cooperative shall indemnify and hold harmless each board member, including Raul Mendes, against any and all claims, losses, damages, or liabilities arising out of or related to the approval, execution, or administration of any workspace sublease agreement, provided that the board member acted in good faith and in the reasonable belief that their actions were in the best interests of the Cooperative. This indemnification shall extend to legal fees and costs incurred in defending against such claims. This provision shall be effective immediately upon adoption and shall apply retroactively to any sublease agreements entered into prior to this amendment.
